subject: ali-yahya

sectors_excited.name: Restaking / cryptoeconomic security (EigenLayer)
sectors_excited.why: Ethereum unbundled consensus from application logic; EigenLayer unbundles cryptoeconomic security itself into a two-sided market. Developers can build decentralized services — novel VMs, oracles, bridges, DA, networks with specialized hardware — on Ethereum's existing validator set rather than bootstrapping a new chain. A market-based mechanism for extending Ethereum. We just led their Series B.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges, and networks with specialized hardware

sectors_excited.name: Rollups, scalability & shared sequencing (Espresso)
sectors_excited.why: The scaling end-game is thousands-to-millions of rollups on Ethereum's bedrock of security. The open problem is fragmentation — each rollup is its own chain, so composability breaks. Shared sequencing is the most compelling fix I've heard: a marketplace that lets sequencers cooperate so rollups interoperate without surrendering independence. We just led Espresso's Series B.

citations.quote: Ethereum’s scalability end-game is all about rollups—potentially thousands or even millions of rollups, each one a mini-ecosystem or an application that is built on top of Ethereum’s bedrock of security.

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge proofs
sectors_excited.why: ZK is the rare building block that simultaneously unlocks scalability (rollups) and privacy (encrypted-but-verifiable state). It will transform the entire web3 stack, and the frontier — Lasso/Jolt, zkEVMs, zkML — is moving faster than almost anyone expected.
sectors_excited.age_note: core thesis since 2022-02, reaffirmed through 2023 (ZK Canon, Lasso/Jolt 'groundbreaking')

citations.quote: Zero Knowledge Proofs will completely transform the web3 tech stack in the coming years

sectors_excited.name: Privacy / encryption on blockchains
sectors_excited.why: Privacy isn't only user protection — it expands the design space. Encrypted, verifiable smart contracts unlock applications transparent chains can't support: games with hidden state, private DeFi, identity. An essential precondition for mainstream adoption (Aztec, Nym, Iron Fish, Aleo).
sectors_excited.age_note: repeated near-verbatim 2021–2022, reaffirmed with Aztec (2022-12) and Aztec Sandbox (2023-09)

citations.quote: Privacy is important for web3, not just to protect user data, but because it unlocks a whole new region of the design space
Privacy unlocks a whole new region of the design space

sectors_excited.name: AI × crypto
sectors_excited.why: My signature lens: AI centralizes, crypto decentralizes — natural counterweights. Crypto can build permissionless markets for the long tail of compute, data, and models (Gensyn); ZK can make AI verifiable and private (zkML); and cryptography gives us proof-of-humanity against a flood of deepfakes. We've barely begun exploring this design space.

citations.quote: Crypto and AI are intersecting to create a design space that we've barely begun to explore.

By tapping into the long tail of idle, ML-capable compute around the world—such as in smaller data centers, gaming computers, and eventually even smartphones—Gensyn has the potential to 10-100x the available compute power for machine learning.

sectors_excited.name: DePIN / community-owned physical networks
sectors_excited.why: Crypto incentives solve the cold-start problem that has always plagued community-owned infrastructure. Helium / Nova Labs is the proof point — the fastest-growing wireless network ever, emerging bottom-up from users rather than top-down from telco giants. A concrete example of crypto going well beyond currency.
sectors_excited.age_note: thesis since 2021-08 (Helium); restated 2022-03 and listed among my 2023 active categories

citations.quote: Helium’s use of crypto incentives spurred the world’s fastest-growing wireless network

sectors_concerned.name: Speculative 'casino' (memecoins, high leverage, airdrop farming)
sectors_concerned.why: The degen casino is the primary force holding the space back — soulless tokens, 100x perps, parasitic airdrop farming, vanity TVL. It is not a necessary bootstrap; it's a distraction from crypto's real purpose: long-term alignment of incentives to scale human cooperation.

citations.quote: Tired AF — arbitrage, 100x leverage, yield farming, flash loans, MEV capture, memecoins, airdrop farming, day-trading, one-off games
We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back.

sectors_concerned.name: Fragmentation of network effects
sectors_concerned.why: The flip side of my excitement about rollups and modularity. Network effects dominate everything; modularity that fragments and weakens them is value-destructive, while modularity that extends them wins. Too many disconnected chains/rollups — or standards mistaken for networks — fracture Ethereum's composability.

citations.quote: Standards have network effects, but they are not networks.
The biggest disadvantage of a rollup-centric future is the fragmentation of the Ethereum ecosystem.

tokens_concerned.name: Memecoins / 'tokens without soul'
tokens_concerned.why: Tokens that moon on memes and then fall off a cliff have no purpose beyond speculation. I want tokens that encode real incentive structures — lockups, rewards for verifiable work, on-chain reputation, iterated games — not soulless pump-and-dump vehicles.

citations.quote: we don't need tokens without soul or purpose that moon on nothing but memes and then fall off a cliff

risk_regime.why: I deliberately under-attend to price and market timing — it's not how I reason. Asset prices fluctuate cycle to cycle while innovation compounds through each one, so I index on the technology, not the tape. As a builder and investor I stay radically optimistic and keep deploying into infrastructure regardless of where the market sits.

citations.quote: The history of crypto shows that asset prices may fluctuate but innovation continues to increase through each cycle.

notes: Two registers, as always: strong technology convictions, deliberately weak price convictions. My throughline is that crypto's value is scaling human cooperation and trust-minimization, not speculation — so I get excited about infrastructure that expands the design space (ZK, privacy, restaking, shared sequencing, AI×crypto, DePIN) and concerned about anything that fragments network effects or turns the space into a zero-sum casino. No single chain or architecture spans the whole tradeoff space; I expect a plural, Ethereum-anchored, modular ecosystem. I don't pick tokens as price bets — that empty bucket is the honest answer, not a gap.
